mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate compresses discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, which directly increases the present value of future equity cash flows and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS level fails to show negative correlation with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, elevated joblessness reduces aggregate household income and consumer spending expectations, which triggers risk-off positioning and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate a broad demand collapse that overwhelms sector-specific resilience, generating strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both decline on a 3-month change basis
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and RSAFS do not produce significantly worse 20-day forward returns than single-series declines (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In the post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal manufacturing restocking and rising capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns with greater than 55% hit rate during 2009-2011.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth captures aggregate income and spending momentum; positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 directly raises corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth does not positively correlate with 60-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, the zero lower bound eliminates conventional monetary policy space and markets price in deflationary risks and financial instability, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls and increases in the unemployment rate confirm a genuine labor market contraction rather than statistical noise, amplifying negative wealth effects and reducing S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS changes and UNRATE changes shows no incremental predictive power (F-test p > 0.05) for 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts serve as a leading cyclical indicator; levels above 600 thousand units signal construction recovery and downstream demand for materials and credit, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST levels above 600k fail to coincide with positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 55% of triggered instances.
